title: Drizzle + SQLite setup with first migration (users, sessions, memberships)
description: Configure Drizzle ORM against a local SQLite file and create the first migration covering the auth-related tables: `users`, `sessions`, `memberships`. Include a seed script that creates the bootstrap admin from `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` if no admin exists yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Drizzle schema file defines `users (id, nome, email, senha_hash, criado_em)`, `sessions (id, user_id, expira_em)`, `memberships (user_id, role)` with `role` constrained to `admin | editor`
Running the migration against a fresh `DATABASE_PATH` creates all three tables with correct columns and constraints
Seed script creates exactly one admin user from `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` only if the `users` table is empty; running it twice does not create duplicates
Seed script never logs the plaintext password

steps.description: Install Drizzle ORM and SQLite driver
steps.details: In `apps/server`, add `drizzle-orm`, `drizzle-kit`, and `better-sqlite3` (or `@libsql/client` if preferring libSQL) as dependencies.

Create `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts` with Drizzle table definitions for `users`, `sessions`, `memberships` matching the PRD's conceptual data model (section 9). Use `text('id').primaryKey()` with UUID default, `text('email').notNull().unique()`, foreign keys via `.references()`.
Create `apps/server/drizzle.config.ts` reading `DATABASE_PATH` from env. Run `drizzle-kit generate` to produce the first SQL migration file under `apps/server/drizzle/`.

steps.description: Add migration runner on server boot
steps.details: In `apps/server/src/db/migrate.ts`, run pending migrations against `DATABASE_PATH` on process start, creating the SQLite file and parent directory if missing.

Create `apps/server/src/db/seed.ts`: check if `users` table is empty; if so, hash `INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` (placeholder hashing call, finalized with argon2 in TASK-9) and insert a user + `memberships` row with `role: 'admin'` using `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`. Never `console.log` the password.

steps.description: Verify against a fresh database file
steps.details: Delete any local `DATABASE_PATH` file, run the migration + seed, and confirm via a Drizzle query that one admin user exists with a `memberships` row of `role = 'admin'`. Run seed a second time and confirm no duplicate is created.

technicalNotes: Use UUID (via `crypto.randomUUID()`) for primary keys rather than autoincrement, to keep IDs stable across future export/import or backup-restore operations.
`memberships` intentionally has no extra scope column beyond `role` — PRD explicitly states '1 instância = 1 design system, sem escopo extra'.

Real password hashing (argon2) lands in TASK-9; this task can use a temporary placeholder hash function if sequencing requires it, but should be wired to the real one before TASK-9 is considered done.
